notes: mistral-vibe `!`-shell escape. vibe runs `!echo hello` LOCALLY (no LLM turn) and writes ONE injected user line to messages.jsonl: `{"role":"user","injected":true,"content":"Manual `!` command result from the user. Use this as context only...."}` (confirmed in 3 live transcripts; source app.py:1574-1580 + agent_loop.py:751-777). The vibe parser maps role:user → user_message UNCONDITIONALLY, not checking `injected` (parser.go:49-56, TestInjectedUser parser_test.go:41-47), so state_classifier rule 4 flips ready→working; and because a bare `!cmd` produces no assistant/turn_done, IsAgentDone() (metrics.go:392-427) stays false → the session STICKS in working after the shell command exits. That violates the scenario's 'state does not stick in working' / step-5 'returns to ready'. KNOWN_FAILING: this spec asserts the CORRECT behavior (mirroring claudecode's intent reading — the shell_escape_window stays ready, no transition to working) so it FAILS on the current vibe parser until it skips/non-activity-marks the injected `!` result (claudecode/parser.go:216-225 is the pattern). The final real turn proves the session is still alive and a real prompt drives ready→working→ready. Single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<ts>_<hash> transcript directory via SessionIDFromPath); session_birth anchored to start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal the birth; record settles the presession-vs-real ordering. Observations: agent=mistral-vibe; model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed from live meta.json config.active_model, passes NormalizeModelName unchanged); tokens_nonzero=true (sidecar stats.context_tokens on turn_done); cost_nonzero=false (adapter reads no session_cost; mistral-medium-3.5 absent from core/pkg/capacity). Driver: gap:wait_turn (baseline column gap — record ports SEAMs 1/2/3 before driving).

text: vibe session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts>_<hash> id, whichever arrives first; single-birth, anchored UNPINNED to start)

KNOWN_FAILING: after `!echo hello` runs locally, the CORRECT behavior is that state stays ready (the injected `!` result is context-only, not a turn). vibe's parser instead treats the injected user line as user activity → ready→working with no turn_done to close it, so the session sticks in working. This phase asserts the correct no-stick behavior and fails on the current parser.
After the shell-escape window, a normal user prompt ('Reply with exactly the word: alive') drives ready → working — proves the session is still alive
Final turn completes back to ready (assistant terminal text → turn_done → ready)
